root_text.an2.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an2.1:2.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco passati coraṁ āgucāriṁ rājāno gahetvā vividhā kammakāraṇā kārente;
root_text.an2.1:2.6: kasāhipi tāḷente, vettehipi tāḷente, addhadaṇḍakehipi tāḷente, hatthampi chindante, pādampi chindante, hatthapādampi chindante, kaṇṇampi chindante, nāsampi chindante, kaṇṇanāsampi chindante, bilaṅgathālikampi karonte, saṅkhamuṇḍikampi karonte, rāhumukhampi karonte, jotimālikampi karonte, hatthapajjotikampi karonte, erakavattikampi karonte, cīrakavāsikampi karonte, eṇeyyakampi karonte, baḷisamaṁsikampi karonte, kahāpaṇikampi karonte, khārāpatacchikampi karonte, palighaparivattikampi karonte, palālapīṭhakampi karonte, tattenapi telena osiñcante, sunakhehipi khādāpente, jīvantampi sūle uttāsente, asināpi sīsaṁ chindante.
root_text.an2.1:3.2: ‘yathārūpānaṁ kho pāpakānaṁ kammānaṁ hetu coraṁ āgucāriṁ rājāno gahetvā vividhā kammakāraṇā kārenti;
root_text.an2.1:3.3: kasāhipi tāḷenti, vettehipi tāḷenti, addhadaṇḍakehipi tāḷenti, hatthampi chindanti, pādampi chindanti, hatthapādampi chindanti, kaṇṇampi chindanti, nāsampi chindanti, kaṇṇanāsampi chindanti, bilaṅgathālikampi karonti, saṅkhamuṇḍikampi karonti, rāhumukhampi karonti, jotimālikampi karonti, hatthapajjotikampi karonti, erakavattikampi karonti, cīrakavāsikampi karonti, eṇeyyakampi karonti, baḷisamaṁsikampi karonti, kahāpaṇikampi karonti, khārāpatacchikampi karonti, palighaparivattikampi karonti, palālapīṭhakampi karonti, tattenapi telena osiñcanti, sunakhehipi khādāpenti, jīvantampi sūle uttāsenti, asināpi sīsaṁ chindanti.
root_text.an2.1:3.4: Ahañceva kho pana evarūpaṁ pāpakammaṁ kareyyaṁ, mampi rājāno gahetvā evarūpā vividhā kammakāraṇā kāreyyuṁ;
root_text.an2.1:3.6: So diṭṭhadhammikassa vajjassa bhīto na paresaṁ pābhataṁ vilumpanto carati.
root_text.an2.1:4.3: ‘kāyaduccaritassa kho pana pāpako dukkho vipāko abhisamparāyaṁ, vacīduccaritassa pāpako dukkho vipāko abhisamparāyaṁ, manoduccaritassa pāpako dukkho vipāko abhisamparāyaṁ.
root_text.an2.1:4.4: Ahañceva kho pana kāyena duccaritaṁ careyyaṁ, vācāya duccaritaṁ careyyaṁ, manasā duccaritaṁ careyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.1:4.5: Kiñca taṁ yāhaṁ na kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.an2.1:4.6: So samparāyikassa vajjassa bhīto kāyaduccaritaṁ pahāya kāyasucaritaṁ bhāveti, vacīduccaritaṁ pahāya vacīsucaritaṁ bhāveti, manoduccaritaṁ pahāya manosucaritaṁ bhāveti, suddhaṁ attānaṁ pariharati.
root_text.an2.1:4.10: ‘diṭṭhadhammikassa vajjassa bhāyissāma, samparāyikassa vajjassa bhāyissāma, vajjabhīruno bhavissāma vajjabhayadassāvino’ti.
root_text.an2.1:4.12: Vajjabhīruno, bhikkhave, vajjabhayadassāvino etaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ yaṁ parimuccissati sabbavajjehī”ti.
root_text.an2.2:1.3: Yañca gihīnaṁ agāraṁ ajjhāvasataṁ cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānuppadānatthaṁ padhānaṁ, yañca agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajitānaṁ sabbūpadhipaṭinissaggatthaṁ padhānaṁ.
root_text.an2.2:1.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, dve padhānāni durabhisambhavāni lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an2.2:2.1: Etadaggaṁ, bhikkhave, imesaṁ dvinnaṁ padhānānaṁ yadidaṁ sabbūpadhipaṭinissaggatthaṁ padhānaṁ.
root_text.an2.3:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa kāyaduccaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti kāyasucaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.3:1.4: vacīduccaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti vacīsucaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.3:1.5: manoduccaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti manosucaritaṁ.
root_text.an2.3:1.6: So ‘kāyaduccaritaṁ me katan’ti tappati, ‘akataṁ me kāyasucaritan’ti tappati;
root_text.an2.3:1.7: ‘vacīduccaritaṁ me katan’ti tappati, ‘akataṁ me vacīsucaritan’ti tappati;
root_text.an2.3:1.8: ‘manoduccaritaṁ me katan’ti tappati, ‘akataṁ me manosucaritan’ti tappati.
root_text.an2.3:1.9: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā tapanīyā”ti.
root_text.an2.4:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa kāyasucaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti kāyaduccaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.4:1.4: vacīsucaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti vacīduccaritaṁ;
root_text.an2.4:1.5: manosucaritaṁ kataṁ hoti, akataṁ hoti manoduccaritaṁ.
root_text.an2.4:1.6: So ‘kāyasucaritaṁ me katan’ti na tappati, ‘akataṁ me kāyaduccaritan’ti na tappati;
root_text.an2.4:1.7: ‘vacīsucaritaṁ me katan’ti na tappati, ‘akataṁ me vacīduccaritan’ti na tappati;
root_text.an2.4:1.8: ‘manosucaritaṁ me katan’ti na tappati, ‘akataṁ me manoduccaritan’ti na tappati.
root_text.an2.4:1.9: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā atapanīyā”ti.
root_text.an2.5:1.2: yā ca asantuṭṭhitā kusalesu dhammesu, yā ca appaṭivānitā padhānasmiṁ.
root_text.an2.5:1.4: ‘kāmaṁ taco ca nhāru ca aṭṭhi ca avasissatu, sarīre upassussatu maṁsalohitaṁ, yaṁ taṁ purisathāmena purisavīriyena purisaparakkamena pattabbaṁ na taṁ apāpuṇitvā vīriyassa saṇṭhānaṁ bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an2.5:1.5: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, appamādādhigatā sambodhi, appamādādhigato anuttaro yogakkhemo.
root_text.an2.5:1.7: ‘kāmaṁ taco ca nhāru ca aṭṭhi ca avasissatu, sarīre upassussatu maṁsalohitaṁ, yaṁ taṁ purisathāmena purisavīriyena purisaparakkamena pattabbaṁ na taṁ apāpuṇitvā vīriyassa saṇṭhānaṁ bhavissatī’ti, tumhepi, bhikkhave, nacirasseva—
root_text.an2.5:1.8: yassatthāya kulaputtā sammadeva agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajanti, tadanuttaraṁ—brahmacariyapariyosānaṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharissatha.
root_text.an2.5:1.11: Kāmaṁ taco ca nhāru ca aṭṭhi ca avasissatu, sarīre upassussatu maṁsalohitaṁ, yaṁ taṁ purisathāmena purisavīriyena purisaparakkamena pattabbaṁ na taṁ apāpuṇitvā vīriyassa saṇṭhānaṁ bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an2.6:1.3: Yā ca saṁyojaniyesu dhammesu assādānupassitā, yā ca saṁyojaniyesu dhammesu nibbidānupassitā.
root_text.an2.6:1.4: Saṁyojaniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu assādānupassī viharanto rāgaṁ na pajahati, dosaṁ na pajahati, mohaṁ na pajahati.
root_text.an2.6:1.5: Rāgaṁ appahāya, dosaṁ appahāya, mohaṁ appahāya na parimuccati jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.an2.6:2.1: Saṁyojaniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu nibbidānupassī viharanto rāgaṁ pajahati, dosaṁ pajahati, mohaṁ pajahati.
root_text.an2.6:2.2: Rāgaṁ pahāya, dosaṁ pahāya, mohaṁ pahāya, parimuccati jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.an2.7:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā kaṇhā”ti.
root_text.an2.8:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve dhammā sukkā”ti.
root_text.an2.9:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve sukkā dhammā lokaṁ na pāleyyuṁ, nayidha paññāyetha mātāti vā mātucchāti vā mātulānīti vā ācariyabhariyāti vā garūnaṁ dārāti vā.
root_text.an2.9:1.5: Sambhedaṁ loko agamissa, yathā ajeḷakā kukkuṭasūkarā soṇasiṅgālā.
root_text.an2.9:1.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, ime dve sukkā dhammā lokaṁ pālenti tasmā paññāyati mātāti vā mātucchāti vā mātulānīti vā ācariyabhariyāti vā garūnaṁ dārāti vā”ti.

translation_text.an2.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an2.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an2.1:2.3: The fault apparent in this life, and the fault to do with lives to come.
translation_text.an2.1:2.4: What is the fault apparent in this life?
translation_text.an2.1:2.5: It’s when someone sees that kings have arrested a bandit, a criminal, and subjected them to various punishments—
translation_text.an2.1:2.6: whipping, caning, and clubbing; cutting off hands or feet, or both; cutting off ears or nose, or both; the ‘porridge pot’, the ‘shell-shave’, the ‘Rāhu’s mouth’, the ‘garland of fire’, the ‘burning hand’, the ‘bulrush twist’, the ‘bark dress’, the ‘antelope’, the ‘meat hook’, the ‘coins’, the ‘caustic pickle’, the ‘twisting bar’, the ‘straw mat’; being splashed with hot oil, being fed to the hounds, being impaled alive, and being beheaded.
translation_text.an2.1:3.2: ‘If I were to commit the kinds of bad deeds for which the kings arrested that bandit, that criminal,
translation_text.an2.1:3.4: the rulers would arrest me and subject me to the same punishments.
translation_text.an2.1:3.6: Afraid of the fault apparent in this life, they do not steal the belongings of others.
translation_text.an2.1:3.7: This is called the fault apparent in this life.
translation_text.an2.1:4.1: What is the fault to do with lives to come?
translation_text.an2.1:4.3: ‘Bad conduct of body, speech, or mind has a bad, painful result in the next life.
translation_text.an2.1:4.5: then, when my body breaks up, after death, won’t I be reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell?’
translation_text.an2.1:4.6: Afraid of the fault to do with lives to come, they give up bad conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, and develop good conduct by way of body, speech, and mind, keeping themselves pure.
translation_text.an2.1:4.7: This is called the fault to do with lives to come.
translation_text.an2.1:4.9: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an2.1:4.10: ‘We will fear the fault apparent in this life, and we will fear the fault to do with lives to come. We will fear faults, seeing the danger in faults.’
translation_text.an2.1:4.12: If you fear faults, seeing the danger in faults, you can expect to be freed from all faults.”
translation_text.an2.2:1.1: “These two endeavors are challenging in the world.
translation_text.an2.2:1.3: The endeavor of laypeople staying in a home to provide robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick. And the endeavor of those gone forth from the lay life to homelessness to let go of all attachments.
translation_text.an2.2:1.4: These are the two endeavors that are challenging in the world.
translation_text.an2.2:2.1: The better of these two endeavors is the effort to let go of all attachments.
translation_text.an2.2:2.2: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an2.2:2.3: ‘We shall endeavor to let go of all attachments.’
translation_text.an2.3:1.3: It’s when someone has done bad things and not done good things, by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an2.3:1.6: Thinking, ‘I’ve done bad things by way of body, speech, and mind’, they’re mortified. Thinking, ‘I haven’t done good things by way of body, speech, and mind’, they’re mortified.
translation_text.an2.3:1.9: These are the two things that are mortifying.”
translation_text.an2.4:1.1: “These two things, mendicants, are not mortifying.
translation_text.an2.4:1.3: It’s when someone has done good things and not done bad things, by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an2.4:1.6: Thinking, ‘I’ve done good things by way of body, speech, and mind’, they’re not mortified. Thinking, ‘I haven’t done bad things by way of body, speech, and mind’, they’re not mortified.
translation_text.an2.4:1.9: These are the two things that are not mortifying.”
translation_text.an2.5:1.1: “Mendicants, I have learned these two things for myself—
translation_text.an2.5:1.2: to never be content with skillful qualities, and to never stop trying.
translation_text.an2.5:1.4: ‘Gladly, let only skin, sinews, and bones remain! Let the flesh and blood waste away in my body! I will not stop trying until I have achieved what is possible by human strength, energy, and vigor.’
translation_text.an2.5:1.5: It was by diligence that I achieved awakening, and by diligence that I achieved the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an2.5:1.6: If you too never stop trying, thinking:
translation_text.an2.5:1.7: ‘Gladly, let only skin, sinews, and bones remain! Let the flesh and blood waste away in my body! I will not stop trying until I have achieved what is possible by human strength, energy, and vigor.’
translation_text.an2.5:1.8: You will soon realize the supreme culmination of the spiritual path in this very life. You will live having achieved with your own insight the goal for which gentlemen rightly go forth from the lay life to homelessness.
translation_text.an2.5:1.9: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an2.5:1.11: “Gladly, let only skin, sinews, and bones remain! Let the flesh and blood waste away in my body! I will not stop trying until I have achieved what is possible by human strength, energy, and vigor.”’
translation_text.an2.6:1.3: Seeing things that tighten the fetters as gratifying, and seeing things that tighten the fetters as boring.
translation_text.an2.6:1.4: When you keep seeing things that tighten the fetters as gratifying, you don’t give up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an2.6:1.5: When these are not given up, you’re not freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.an2.6:1.6: You’re not freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.an2.6:2.1: When you keep seeing things that tighten the fetters as boring, you give up greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an2.6:2.2: When these are given up, you’re freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.an2.6:2.3: You’re freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.an2.7:1.4: These are the two things that are dark.”
translation_text.an2.8:1.4: These are the two things that are bright.”
translation_text.an2.9:1.1: “These two bright things, mendicants, protect the world.
translation_text.an2.9:1.4: If these two bright things did not protect the world, there would be no recognition of the status of mother, aunts, or wives and partners of tutors and respected people.
translation_text.an2.9:1.5: The world would become dissolute, like goats and sheep, chickens and pigs, and hounds and jackals.
translation_text.an2.9:1.6: But because the two bright things protect the world, there is recognition of the status of mother, aunts, and wives and partners of tutors and respected people.”
translation_text.an2.10:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these two entries to the rainy season.
translation_text.an2.10:1.4: These are the two entries to the rainy season.”

variant_text.an2.1:2.5: vividhā kammakāraṇā → vividhā kammakaraṇā (sya-all, pts1ed); vividhāni kammakaraṇāni (mr)
variant_text.an2.1:2.6: khārāpatacchikampi → khārāpaṭicchakampi (sya-all, km, mr) | palālapīṭhakampi → palālapiṭṭhikampi (bj)
